etymology.description: 亼(亼) (jié, "to gather") + two 兄(兄) (xiōng, "elder brother") -> people answering together or unanimously -> stylized as 亼(亼) (jié, "to gather") + 吅(吅) (xuān, "speak aloud") + 从(从) (cóng, "from")
etymology.interpretation: The traditional form 僉 (qiān, "all") represents unity and agreement, as it depicts an assembly of people answering together, emphasized by the elements for gathering, speaking aloud collectively, and the idea of coming together.
etymology.simplification: 佥 is a simplified form of 僉, retaining only 亼(亼) (jié, "to gather") with a single component for streamlining the idea of consensus.

examples.chinese: 佥在场的人(僉在場的人)都同意了这个建议(都同意了這個建議)。
examples.pinyin: Qiān zàichǎng de rén dōu tóngyìle zhège jiànyì.
examples.english: All the people present agreed with this suggestion.

examples.chinese: 我们佥认为(我們僉認為)，这个方案是最好的(這個方案是最好的)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen qiān rènwéi, zhège fāng'àn shì zuì hǎo de.
examples.english: We all believe that this plan is the best.

examples.chinese: 佥都知道(僉都知道)，他是一个诚实的人(他是一個誠實的人)。
examples.pinyin: Qiān dōu zhīdào, tā shì yīgè chéngshí de rén.
examples.english: Everyone knows that he is an honest person.
